verification_report.summary: The proof correctly proves that every finite group of prime order is cyclic. It states the hypothesis, picks a non-identity element, considers the cyclic subgroup it generates, applies Lagrange's theorem to deduce the subgroup order divides the prime p, uses the primality of p to conclude the subgroup order must be p (since it is not 1), and therefore the subgroup equals the whole group, showing the group is cyclic. All reasoning steps are logically valid, no gaps or errors exist, and all assumptions are used correctly.

The proof correctly proves that every finite group of prime order is cyclic. The proof picks a non-identity element g, considers its cyclic subgroup ⟨g⟩, applies Lagrange's theorem to deduce |g| divides |G| = p, uses primality of p to conclude |g| = p (since |g| ≥ 2), and therefore ⟨g⟩ = G, showing G is cyclic. All steps are logically valid, no gaps or errors exist. Supporting lemmas (Lagrange's theorem application, prime divisor definition) are stated and proven. The theorem appears last as required with the original statement preserved.
